matches.moment: A rotated France backline missing Saliba is undone by a Kane set-piece header to level it, but Mbappé's cool finish from a rare clear sight of goal in a mutually loose, rotated game restores the lead for good.

verdict: A third-place game between two heartbroken favorites was never going to be a defensive chess match, and the numbers back that up: France's 2.04 xG/game and 0.80 xGA/game against a tighter opponent-quality band outstrip England's 1.56 xG/game and leakier ~1.40 xGA/game versus comparable knockout-stage opposition, while an extra day of recovery and Mbappé's live Golden Boot tiebreaker against Messi give Les Bleus the sharper edge in motivation despite both sides rotating heavily. England counter with proven end-product in Kane and Bellingham (six goals apiece) against a France backline missing William Saliba, and the historical base rate for these fixtures — 16 of the last 20 finishing over 2.5 goals, both teams scoring in 14 of them — argues strongly against a cautious script. The likeliest outcome is exactly that: an open, rotated, high-event game that France's superior underlying numbers and rest edge nose them through, while England leave Miami with pride, goals, and no silverware. — The Analyst.

players.name: Kylian Mbappé
players.stat: 8 goals this tournament, 16 shots on target, 5.48 shots/appearance
players.watch: Needs two goal involvements to overtake Messi's assist tiebreak and win the Golden Boot outright

players.name: Harry Kane
players.stat: 6 goals this tournament, brace vs DR Congo in Round of 32
players.watch: Aerial threat against a rotated, Saliba-less France center-back pairing

players.name: Jude Bellingham
players.stat: 6 goals this tournament, tied with Kane for England's team lead
players.watch: Late box arrivals against a tired, rotated France midfield

players.name: Désiré Doué
players.stat: Regular starter across France's knockout run (tournament appearance data; club-level output not separately tracked here)
players.watch: Pace in behind an England back line reshuffled by Reece James' fitness doubt
